Review Title: Finally done replacing cheap rain jackets
Review Content: I used to buy a new rain jacket every fall because the cheap ones always soaked through by January. This is my second season with the Cascade and it still beads water like day one. Should have spent the $149 a long time ago.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Worth every penny
Review Content: Great jacket. Keeps me dry on my weekend hikes. That's all I needed it to do.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: My Saturday morning jacket
Review Content: I hike most weekends and this has become the jacket I grab without thinking about it. Keeps rain out and I don't overheat on the uphill. The Slate color doesn't show dirt which is a plus.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Runs a little big
Review Content: Quality is great but the medium is roomier than I expected. I'm 5'10 170 and there's extra room in the torso. Fine for layering a fleece underneath but if you want a closer fit you might size down.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Replaced my old leaky jacket and couldn't be happier
Review Content: My last rain jacket was a $40 one from a big box store that started leaking after about six months. A friend recommended this one and the difference is night and day. Completely dry after a two-hour hike in steady rain.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Quiet fabric is a big deal
Review Content: This doesn't make that loud swishing sound when you walk. I know that sounds minor but it was driving me crazy with my old jacket. This one is soft and quiet and actually keeps rain out. Win all around.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Good jacket but wish it had a chest pocket
Review Content: The hand pockets sit right where my backpack hip belt goes so they're useless on the trail. A chest pocket for my phone would make this perfect. Waterproofing and comfort are great though.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Five stars. Works as described.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Best rain jacket I've owned at this price
Review Content: I've tried jackets at this price point before and they always felt plasticky or trapped moisture inside. This one breathes well enough that I don't feel clammy and the pit zips help on warmer days. Really solid for $149.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Waterproofing faded after a few months
Review Content: This jacket was great at first. Water rolled right off. But after maybe three months of regular weekend use the shoulders started absorbing water instead of shedding it. I washed it and threw it in the dryer like the tag says and it improved a bit but I'm not thrilled.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Perfect for dog walks and day hikes
Review Content: I wear this almost every day from October through April. Dog walks in the morning and hikes on the weekend. Packs down small enough to shove in my daypack when the sun comes out. Fir Green color is great.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Bought two. One for me and one for my dad.
Review Content: My dad hikes every weekend in North Carolina and has been wearing garbage rain gear forever. Bought him this for his birthday and now he won't stop talking about it. Had to get one for myself.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Leaked through the seams on my third wear
Review Content: Water came through the shoulder seams on a moderate rainy hike. Brand new jacket. Returned it. Maybe I got a defective one but that's a bad first impression.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Solid but the zipper needs some break-in
Review Content: The main zipper is stiff when the jacket is new. Takes some effort with cold hands. It's getting smoother with use though. Everything else about the jacket is really good.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: This is all the rain jacket most people need
Review Content: I'm not a gear person. I just hike on weekends and needed something that works when it rains. This works. It's comfortable and I don't have to think about it. That's exactly what I wanted.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Too boxy for slim builds
Review Content: I'm 5'8 and 145 and even the small was way too roomy. The fabric quality is nice but the fit didn't work for me at all. Returned it. Would love to see slimmer sizing or an XS.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Survived a full day of rain in Vermont
Review Content: Took this on a fall hiking trip in Vermont and we got rained on for about six straight hours. I stayed dry the whole time. Pockets kept my phone and snacks dry too. No complaints.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Love it
Review Content: Exactly what I was looking for. Lightweight and waterproof. Wears great over a hoodie.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Two years in and still going
Review Content: I bought this two seasons ago and it's held up through a ton of rainy weekends. Seams are fine and it still sheds water after washing. For the price I'm really impressed with how long it's lasting.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Does the job but breathability is just okay
Review Content: It keeps rain out really well. On steep uphill stretches I do get a bit sweaty inside but the vents under the arms help. For a $149 jacket I think this is a fair tradeoff. Not a dealbreaker for me.
Product Description: Built for misty mornings and surprise squalls. Keeps you dry without feeling clammy.

Review Title: Carried this on a 4-day trip. No complaints.
Review Content: Loaded it up to about 30 lbs for a long weekend trip and the hip belt carried the weight really well. My back wasn't sore at the end of the day which is more than I can say for my old pack.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Great pack
Review Content: Fits everything I need for a 2-3 night trip. Comfortable to carry. Happy with it.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: 45 liters is the perfect size
Review Content: I can fit my tent and sleeping bag and pad and food for three days without having to cram everything in. The side pockets hold water bottles and I can reach them while walking. Exactly what I needed.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Comfortable but could use more pockets inside
Review Content: The carry is great and I can wear it all day without pain. But inside it's basically one big compartment. I had to buy some stuff sacks to keep things organized. A couple internal dividers or pockets would be nice.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Upgraded from a cheap pack and the difference is huge
Review Content: I was using a $60 pack from a sporting goods store for the last two years. Shoulder straps dug in and the hip belt was a joke. This thing actually transfers weight to my hips. I can hike longer and feel way less beat up at the end of the day.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: The hip belt pockets actually fit a phone
Review Content: Small thing but it matters: I can fit my phone in the hip belt pocket without it sticking out. Snacks on the other side. I don't have to stop and dig through my pack to check my map anymore.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Didn't fit my longer torso well
Review Content: I'm on the taller side and even at the longest setting the pack sits a little low on me. The weight doesn't transfer to my hips the way it should at heavier loads. Might work great for average or shorter torsos though.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: My wife borrowed it and won't give it back
Review Content: I bought this for a backpacking trip. My wife tried it on and took it on her own trip. She refuses to return it so now I'm ordering a second one.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Well built. No unnecessary bells and whistles.
Review Content: I like that this pack doesn't have a bunch of straps and buckles I'll never use. It's simple and everything on it serves a purpose. Comfortable carry and the fabric feels tough.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Water bottles can pop out on steep downhills
Review Content: The side pockets are a good size but on steep descents my bottles have popped out twice. I started using a small bungee to keep them in. Otherwise really happy with the pack.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Solid pack for the price
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Hip belt padding went flat after one trip
Review Content: I did a five-day trip with maybe 35 lbs and by day three the hip belt was noticeably less padded. Last two days were uncomfortable. Disappointing for a $200 pack.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Good midrange option
Review Content: Nothing flashy but it does the job. Frame keeps the load stable on uneven ground and the back panel breathes okay. Competes well with packs that cost more.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Used it in Colorado all summer
Review Content: Took this on probably eight or nine weekend trips in the Rockies this summer. Rain and dust and sun. Held up great. The water-resistant coating on the outside kept the fabric from getting soggy when it rained.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Trekking pole stow system is smart
Review Content: I use poles every hike and this pack has a good attachment system for them. I can collapse them and clip them on without fussing with bungees. Easy to do with one hand.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Stitching started unraveling on the shoulder strap
Review Content: After a few months of regular use the stitching on one shoulder strap started coming apart where it connects to the pack. I expect better durability at this price. Reaching out to their warranty team.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Perfect for weekend overnights
Review Content: This is my go-to for one and two night trips. 45 liters is enough space without encouraging me to overpack. Comfortable for the 5-8 mile approaches I usually do.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Top pocket could be bigger
Review Content: The lid pocket on top gets crowded if you put your snacks and map and permits in there. I wish it was about 50% bigger. The main compartment and overall comfort are great though.
Product Description: Big enough for food and layers and a shelter but not so big that it tempts you to overpack.

Review Title: Actually waterproof. What a concept.
Review Content: I've been burned by cheap tents that leaked in anything more than a drizzle. This one handled steady rain all night and the floor was bone dry in the morning. At 5 lbs it's not the lightest tent out there but for $299 I'll take the extra durability.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Easy to set up even by myself
Review Content: Goes up in about five minutes. I set it up alone at camp and didn't struggle with it. The clip system makes sense and the rainfly goes on logically. No PhD required.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Tight for two people but fine solo
Review Content: My girlfriend and I fit but it's cozy with two sleeping pads side by side. Solo with all my gear spread out it feels roomy. Build quality is really good for a $299 tent.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Surprised by the vestibule space
Review Content: Each side has a vestibule big enough for a backpack and boots. I've used more expensive tents with less covered storage space. Nice touch.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Excellent
Review Content: Great tent. Used it a bunch of times now. Keeps rain out and keeps bugs out. No issues.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Gets condensation inside in cold weather
Review Content: When it's cold and I close up the rainfly I get moisture on the inside walls. Opening a vent helps but then cold air comes in. Not unusual for tents at this price but something to know about.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Best tent I've found for the money
Review Content: I looked at tents at two and three times this price and honestly the Alpine Meadow does everything I need. I'm not counting grams. I just need a tent that keeps me dry on weekend trips and this does it.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Good tent but the stuff sack is way too small
Review Content: Every morning packing this thing back into the included stuff sack is a battle. I bought a bigger compression sack and problem solved. The tent itself is great.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Made me feel confident on my first backpacking trip
Review Content: I'm new to backpacking and this tent was easy to figure out and kept me dry and comfortable all weekend. At 6'1 I fit with room to spare. The little interior pockets for my headlamp and phone are really handy.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Third thing I've bought from Great Outdoors Co.
Review Content: I have the rain shell and the daypack too. This tent is the best thing they make. The rainfly feels solid and the aluminum poles are a big upgrade over the cheap fiberglass poles you get on budget tents.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: A pole snapped on me in wind
Review Content: One of the poles broke at a connection point during a windy night. It wasn't a crazy storm or anything. I splinted it with a repair sleeve to get through the night but that shouldn't happen on a tent that's only a few months old.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.

Review Title: Great tent but I'd recommend a footprint
Review Content: The floor fabric feels like it could get punctured on rocky ground. I've started putting a footprint under it just in case. Everything else about the tent is solid and I'd recommend it to anyone doing weekend trips.
Product Description: A lightweight two-person backpacking tent for three-season use.
